questions.q: At what minimum age may an Ohio resident apply for a Temporary Instruction Permit Identification Card (TIPIC) to begin driving practice?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Licensing - TIPIC / Graduated Driver Licensing
questions.explanation: Ohio issues the temporary instruction permit (TIPIC) starting at age 15 1/2, allowing supervised driving practice.

questions.q: How long must a driver under age 18 hold the temporary instruction permit before taking the road test for a probationary license?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Graduated Driver Licensing - Holding Period
questions.explanation: Minors must hold the TIPIC for a minimum of six months and meet training requirements before the road test.

questions.q: A Temporary Instruction Permit Identification Card (TIPIC) is valid for how long from the date it is issued?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Licensing - TIPIC Validity
questions.explanation: The temporary permit is valid for one year and can be renewed if more practice time is needed.

questions.q: While driving with a temporary instruction permit, the permit holder must be accompanied by a licensed driver who is at least:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Graduated Driver Licensing - Supervision
questions.explanation: A permit holder must drive with a licensed driver age 21 or older (or a parent/guardian) seated beside them.

questions.q: What is the minimum age at which an Ohio teen may obtain a Probationary Driver License?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Graduated Driver Licensing - Probationary License
questions.explanation: After meeting the holding and training requirements, a teen may receive a probationary license at age 16.

questions.q: During the first 12 months of a probationary license, how many non-family passengers under age 21 may the driver carry?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Probationary License - Passenger Restriction
questions.explanation: For the first year, a probationary driver may have only one passenger under 21 who is not a family member.

questions.q: Under Ohio's nighttime driving restriction for a probationary license holder, driving is generally prohibited between:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Probationary License - Nighttime Restriction
questions.explanation: Probationary drivers may not drive from midnight to 6 a.m. except for work, school, or emergency purposes.

questions.q: To meet the vision screening for an Ohio license, an applicant must have at least what vision in one eye (with or without correction)?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Licensing - Vision Screening
questions.explanation: Ohio's vision standard requires at least 20/40 acuity in one eye, with corrective lenses permitted.

questions.q: For the on-road driving test, the vehicle provided by the applicant must have:
options: Only a valid driver's license for the tester
Valid plates, registration, insurance, and working safety equipment
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Road Test - Vehicle Requirements
questions.explanation: The test vehicle must be legally registered, insured, and equipped with working lights, brakes, and signals.

questions.q: A new Ohio resident with a valid out-of-state license must obtain an Ohio driver license within how many days of establishing residency?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Licensing - New Residents
questions.explanation: New residents must transfer their out-of-state license to an Ohio license within 30 days of becoming a resident.

questions.q: An eight-sided red sign always means:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - Regulatory Signs
questions.explanation: The octagonal red sign is the universal STOP sign requiring a full stop.

questions.q: A downward-pointing triangle sign means you must:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - Warning/Yield
questions.explanation: The triangular yield sign requires you to slow and yield to traffic with the right-of-way.

questions.q: A standard red, circular sign with a horizontal white bar means:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - Regulatory Signs
questions.explanation: The circular 'DO NOT ENTER' sign prohibits entering the road from that direction.

questions.q: A yellow pennant-shaped sign is used to warn of:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - No-Passing Zone
questions.explanation: The yellow pennant marks the beginning of a no-passing zone on the left side of the road.

questions.q: A five-sided (pentagonal) sign warns of:
options: A school zone or school crossing
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - School Signs
questions.explanation: Pentagonal signs are used for school advance and school crossing warnings.

questions.q: A round yellow sign warns of:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - Railroad Warning
questions.explanation: The round yellow sign is the advance warning for a railroad crossing.

questions.q: A steady yellow traffic light means:
options: Clear the intersection; prepare to stop
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - Traffic Lights
questions.explanation: A yellow signal tells drivers the light is about to turn red, so clear the intersection safely.

questions.q: A flashing red traffic signal must be treated as:
options: A stop sign (stop, then proceed when clear)
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - Flashing Signals
questions.explanation: A flashing red light has the same meaning as a stop sign: stop, then go when safe.

questions.q: A flashing yellow traffic signal means:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - Flashing Signals
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means slow down and proceed with caution.

questions.q: At a steady red light, when may a driver turn right after stopping?
options: After stopping and yielding, unless a sign prohibits it
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Traffic Signals and Signs - Right Turns on Red
questions.explanation: Ohio permits a right turn on red after a full stop and when no sign bans the turn.

questions.q: Who has the right-of-way at an intersection with no traffic signals or signs?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Right-of-Way - Uncontrolled Intersections
questions.explanation: At uncontrolled intersections, yield to the vehicle on your right.

questions.q: When entering a roundabout, a driver must:
options: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Right-of-Way - Roundabouts
questions.explanation: Traffic in the roundabout has priority; entering drivers yield to it.

questions.q: When approaching an emergency vehicle with flashing lights and siren, a driver should:
options: Pull to the right and stop
Stay in the lane and slow
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Right-of-Way - Emergency Vehicles
questions.explanation: You must yield by moving to the right and stopping until the emergency vehicle passes.

questions.q: When a school bus has red flashing lights and an extended stop arm, drivers approaching from either direction must:
options: Stop only if behind the bus
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Right-of-Way - School Buses
questions.explanation: Ohio law requires stopping at least 10 feet from a bus with red lights and stop arm active.

questions.q: On a divided highway with a physical barrier, drivers on the opposite side of a stopped school bus with flashing red lights:
options: Must stop only if children are present
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Right-of-Way - School Buses (Divided Highway)
questions.explanation: On a physically divided roadway, only traffic traveling the same direction as the bus must stop.

questions.q: Unless otherwise posted, what is the general speed limit in an urban residential or business district in Ohio?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Speed Limits - Urban Districts
questions.explanation: Ohio's default speed limit in municipal residential/business areas is 25 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.q: Unless otherwise posted, what is the basic speed limit on most rural state highways and county roads in Ohio?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Speed Limits - Rural Highways
questions.explanation: The default rural highway limit in Ohio is 55 mph unless posted higher.

questions.q: On limited-access, divided highways (such as interstates) in Ohio, the maximum posted speed limit may be up to:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Speed Limits - Interstate Highways
questions.explanation: Ohio allows up to a 70 mph limit on certain divided, limited-access rural interstate highways.

questions.q: Ohio's basic speed law requires drivers to:
options: Drive at a speed reasonable and prudent for conditions
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Speed Limits - Basic Speed Law
questions.explanation: Even below the posted limit, you must slow to a safe speed for weather, visibility, and traffic.

questions.q: A solid yellow line on your side of the centerline means:
options: The lane is for turns only
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Lane Markings - Yellow Lines
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side prohibits crossing it to pass.

questions.q: Double solid yellow lines in the center of the road mean:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Lane Markings - Double Yellow
questions.explanation: Double solid yellow lines forbid crossing to pass from either direction.

questions.q: A broken (dashed) white line separates lanes of traffic moving:
options: In the same direction (lane changes permitted)
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Lane Markings - White Lines
questions.explanation: Dashed white lines separate same-direction lanes where lane changes are allowed.

questions.q: A solid white line at the edge of the roadway marks:
options: The right edge of the travel lane
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Lane Markings - Solid White
questions.explanation: Solid white edge lines show the boundary of the roadway shoulder.

questions.q: You may not pass another vehicle within how many feet of a railroad crossing?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Passing - Near Railroads
questions.explanation: Ohio prohibits passing within 100 feet of a railroad crossing.

questions.q: You may not pass on a hill or curve unless you can see at least how far ahead?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Passing - Visibility
questions.explanation: Only pass when you can see at least 500 feet of clear road ahead.

questions.q: When must you dim your high-beam headlights to low beams for an oncoming vehicle?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Lights - High Beams
questions.explanation: Switch to low beams within 500 feet of oncoming traffic to avoid blinding others.

questions.q: When following another vehicle, you must use low-beam headlights within how many feet?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Lights - Following
questions.explanation: Use low beams when you are within 200 feet behind another vehicle.

questions.q: Ohio law requires headlights to be on:
options: From sunset to sunrise and in poor visibility
Only when it is raining hard
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Lights - Required Use
questions.explanation: Headlights are required from dusk to dawn and whenever you cannot see 1,000 feet ahead.

questions.q: You must turn on your headlights whenever you cannot see a person or vehicle on the highway at least:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Lights - Visibility Standard
questions.explanation: If visibility is under 1,000 feet, Ohio requires headlights on.

questions.q: A driver must signal a turn or lane change at least how far in advance?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Signaling - Distance
questions.explanation: Ohio requires a signal at least 100 feet before turning or changing lanes.

questions.q: A driver age 21 or older may be arrested for OVI in Ohio at a blood alcohol concentration (BAC) of:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - OVI / Alcohol - Adult Limit
questions.explanation: Ohio's per se OVI limit for adult drivers is a BAC of 0.08% or above.

questions.q: Under Ohio's implied consent law, refusing a chemical (breath/blood/urine) test after a lawful arrest can result in:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - OVI - Implied Consent
questions.explanation: Refusing the test triggers an administrative license suspension under implied consent.

questions.q: In Ohio, who is required by law to wear a safety belt?
options: The driver and all front-seat passengers
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Occupant Protection - Seat Belts
questions.explanation: Ohio requires the driver and front-seat occupants to be properly restrained.

questions.q: Ohio's child passenger safety law requires children under age 4 or under 40 pounds to ride in:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Occupant Protection - Child Restraints
questions.explanation: Young/light children must be in an approved child restraint system.

questions.q: Children ages 4 through 7 who have outgrown a car seat must use:
options: A booster seat until 4'9" tall or age 8
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Occupant Protection - Booster Seats
questions.explanation: Ohio requires a booster seat until the child reaches age 8 or 4 feet 9 inches.

questions.q: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant in Ohio?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Parking - Fire Hydrants
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited within 10 feet of a fire hydrant.

questions.q: You may not park within how many feet of a stop sign or traffic signal?
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Parking - Signs/Signals
questions.explanation: Keep at least 30 feet from stop signs and traffic signals when parking.

questions.q: The 'three-second rule' is used to maintain a safe:
options: Following distance behind the vehicle ahead
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Safe Driving - Following Distance
questions.explanation: The three-second rule helps keep a safe gap behind the car ahead.

questions.q: When stopping behind another vehicle at a traffic light, you should:
options: Pull right up to its bumper
Leave room to see its rear tires (pull-back space)
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Safe Driving - Stopping Position
questions.explanation: Leave space so you can pull around if needed and see the tires meet pavement.

questions.q: In work zones, fines for traffic violations are typically:
questions.ref: OH BMV Driver Manual - Special Zones - Work Zones
questions.explanation: Ohio doubles fines in marked construction/work zones to protect workers.
